name: Frozen White Chocolate Mousse Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, mix crumbs and melted butter. Press into the bottom of an 8-inch diameter springform pan. Refrigerate while preparing the mousse.
2. Melt 6 oz white chocolate in a double boiler set over simmering water, stirring until smooth. Cool slightly.
3. Bring sugar and water to boil in a heavy medium saucepan, stirring until sugar dissolves. Continue boiling without stirring until a candy thermometer registers 238°F (soft-ball stage).
4. Using an electric mixer fitted with clean dry beaters, beat egg whites with cream of tartar in a large bowl until soft peaks form.
5. Gradually beat hot syrup into the egg whites. Continue beating until the mixture is stiff and glossy and the bottom of the bowl cools to barely lukewarm (about 3 minutes).
6. Fold in the warm chocolate.
7. Refrigerate the chocolate mixture until cool but not set, about 5 minutes.
8. Whip cream, Grand Marnier, and vanilla in another large bowl until soft peaks form. Gently fold this into the chocolate mixture.
9. Fold in chopped white chocolate. Pour the mixture into the crust; smooth the top with a spatula. Sprinkle with chopped pistachios.
10. Freeze until firm, about 6 hours. (Can be prepared up to 3 days ahead; cover tightly.) Remove pan sides.
11. Cut the dessert into wedges. Serve with raspberry sauce.
Raspberry Sauce:
2 cups Fresh or frozen raspberries
1/4 cup Sugar
1 tbsp Grand Marnier liqueur
Mix the raspberries in a food processor or blender, then strain to remove seeds. Stir in sugar and Grand Marnier. Refrigerate until well chilled, about 1 hour. (Can be prepared up to 3 days ahead; cover.)
